figures.caption: Fig. 1: Measurements at Mount Etna. The TDLAS sensor (left) measures either against the handheld reflector in the background or the smaller drone with the spherical reflector. The larger drone carries an in-situ sensor.

Fig. 2: The top image depicts the in-situ sensor that can be mounted on a drone or carried by hand. The tape protects the sensor from the acidic volcanic environment. The bottom images show our two-component remote gas sensing system. The TDLAS sensor (left), model Beamonics BeamSight, measures the gas concentration between itself and the reflector mounted on the drone (right).

Fig. 4: Plot (a) shows the single open-path measurements using the handheld reflector. We used the measurements to calculate the gas distribution map in (b). Plot (c) shows the wind-compensated reconstruction from the data collected with the flying reflector. Both reconstruction plots include the gas detections of the in-situ sensors during the walking tour, as well as the single flying detection shown in Fig. 3. The background map depicts a photogrammetry of the mud volcanoes.
